title: Hattie the Mouse's One-Step-at-a-Time Bedtime
summary: Hattie the Mouse has a new baby sister, which means bedtime is a little bit different than it used to be. Hattie decides to take her nighttime routine one step at a time. After playing with Legos, putting on her shirt, and donning a handmade hat, she settles in to read 'The Mystery Cozy Witch'—a story about getting comfortable on a rug with a bug. Following a sweet lullaby from her mama and the activation of her special squeaker machine, Hattie tucks herself into her crib with a blanket and a sleep sack for a long night of rest.

highlightQuote.text: No, because mice don't wear diapers.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh no, we're gonna close the window because it's bedtime.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I want to leave it open so I can see.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You can look out for a minute. What do you see out there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: All kinds of things.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Like what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Cars and stuff.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Cars and stuff? Okay. Well, I got the stars set up and I'm gonna turn off the light. Okay, now it's time for stars and stories, so please close the window. Thank you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Tonight's stars and stories...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's going to be about Hattie the Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's always about Hattie, indeed. And it's gonna be a short one.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wait, I need to jump on the lines through onto the chair.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, we're doing stars and stories now.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But right now I want to jump into bed.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, how about I fly you into the chair?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no I don't want to go in the chair.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I'll help you. That's not enough space. Okay, I'll move this down to the floor.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Jump. Okay. And now, stars and stories about Hattie the Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Hattie the Mouse had a big problem at bedtime.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, no problems.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie the Mouse had a really nice, relaxing bedtime. And what did she do at her bedtime?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She had a new baby sister, so she did it different.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, she had a new baby sister, so she did it different. How did she do it this time?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She went one step at a time.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's always a good way to do things, one step at a time. So, what was the first step for Hattie's bedtime? Did she play with Legos?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. She played with Legos and then she went to bed.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and then she went to bed. Did she brush her teeth?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, she did not.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Did she put on a diaper?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, because mice don't wear diapers.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're right. Mice don't wear diapers. Or anything else? But they just wear shirts.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and sometimes they wear hats, because Hattie makes them. And Hattie read one book before bed. What book did Hattie read?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The Mystery Cozy Witch.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The Mystery Cozy Witch? Oh, what happens in that book?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She gets cozy on a rug and a bug.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Does it have a happy ending?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Sounds like a nice book. And off the story went in it too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Hattie the Mouse read her one book, and then... um... her mama sang her one song. What was her song, her lullaby?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Little Hattie, time to sleep. Little Hattie, time to sleep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then Hattie went in her crib and turned out the light. Does she have a sound machine? That goes shhh?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No... No, it does have one.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It goes like [whooshing sound].

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, it doesn't, it goes 'squeak squeak car'. No, it's a squeaker machine.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see, it has a squeaker machine because she's a mouse. And does she have a blanket?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And a pillow?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and a sleep sack.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And a sleep sack. And then she put her head down on her pillow, she covered up herself with her blanket...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And her sleep sack.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And she got her sleep sack on, and then she went to sleep. Zzzzz. And that's the end of the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. Go on then.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Baby Sister
characters.description: Hattie's new sibling, whose arrival inspired Hattie to change her bedtime routine.

characters.name: The Mystery Cozy Witch
characters.description: The protagonist of Hattie's favorite bedtime book, who likes to get cozy on a rug with a bug.

characters.name: Mama Mouse
characters.description: The devoted matriarch of the Blind Mice family and watchful guardian of the household, she balances the sweet lilt of nightly lullabies with the adrenaline of a seasoned racer. Behind the wheel of her medium-sized black car—carrying a well-thumbed book on the secrets of junk—she guards Hattie and Avery’s peace as fiercely as she commands the asphalt.

characters.name: Bug
characters.description: A character mentioned in Hattie's bedtime book who gets cozy with the witch.

places.name: Crib
places.description: The cozy place where Hattie the Mouse sleeps with her blanket and pillow.

places.name: Rug
places.description: The setting in Hattie's book where the Mystery Cozy Witch gets comfortable.
